name: create
context: Create pull requests via POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls or gh pr create
info: Create pull requests from feature branches or forks into a base branch. Supports draft PRs, closing keywords for auto-linking [[github/issues]], reviewer assignment, and label application. Use the GitHub REST API `POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` via @octokit/rest or the `gh pr create` CLI command. PRs trigger CI checks from [[github/actions]] and are gated by branch protection rules in [[github/repos/settings]]. See [[github/auth]] for authentication.
body:
# Create Pull Request

Create a PR to propose changes from a head branch into a base branch.

## gh CLI

```bash
# Interactive PR creation
gh pr create

# Non-interactive with all options
gh pr create \
  --title "feat: add user authentication" \
  --body "## Summary
Adds JWT-based authentication with refresh tokens.

Fixes #42

## Test Plan
- [x] Unit tests pass
- [x] Integration tests pass" \
  --base main \
  --head feature/auth \
  --reviewer reviewer-1,reviewer-2 \
  --assignee @me \
  --label enhancement,auth \
  --milestone "v2.0"

# Create a draft PR
gh pr create --title "WIP: new feature" --body "Work in progress" --draft

# Create from a fork
gh pr create --title "Fix typo" --body "Fixes typo in README" --repo upstream/repo
```

## REST API

```bash
# Create via curl
curl -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
  https://api.github.com/repos/owner/repo/pulls \
  -d '{
    "title": "feat: add user authentication",
    "body": "Adds JWT-based authentication.\n\nFixes #42",
    "head": "feature/auth",
    "base": "main",
    "draft": false
  }'
```

```javascript
import { Octokit } from "@octokit/rest";
const octokit = new Octokit({ auth: process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN });

// Create PR
const { data: pr } = await octokit.pulls.create({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  title: "feat: add user authentication",
  body: "## Summary\nAdds JWT-based authentication.\n\nFixes #42\n\n## Test Plan\n- [x] Unit tests pass\n- [x] Integration tests pass",
  head: "feature/auth",
  base: "main",
  draft: false,
});
console.log(pr.number, pr.html_url, pr.mergeable);

// Request reviewers after creation
await octokit.pulls.requestReviewers({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  pull_number: pr.number,
  reviewers: ["reviewer-1", "reviewer-2"],
  team_reviewers: ["security-team"],
});

// Add labels
await octokit.issues.addLabels({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  issue_number: pr.number,  // PRs use issue number for labels
  labels: ["enhancement", "auth"],
});
```

## Cross-Fork PRs

When creating a PR from a fork, use `username:branch` as the head:

```javascript
const { data: pr } = await octokit.pulls.create({
  owner: "upstream-org",
  repo: "upstream-repo",
  title: "Fix: resolve race condition",
  head: "my-username:fix/race-condition",
  base: "main",
});
```

## Closing Keywords

Include these in the PR body to auto-close [[github/issues]] on merge:
- `Fixes #42`, `Closes #42`, `Resolves #42`
- Multiple: `Fixes #42, fixes #43`
- Cross-repo: `Fixes owner/repo#42`

sections.title: gh pr create
sections.body: Use `gh pr create` for the simplest PR creation workflow. Supports `--title`, `--body`, `--base`, `--head`, `--draft`, `--reviewer`, `--assignee`, `--label`, and `--milestone` flags. Omit flags for interactive mode. Automatically detects the current branch as head and the default branch as base. Reads PR templates from `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`.

sections.title: REST API creation
sections.body: POST to `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` with `title`, `head`, `base`, and optional `body`, `draft`, `maintainer_can_modify`. The response includes `number`, `html_url`, `mergeable`, `mergeable_state`, and `state`. For cross-fork PRs, head format is `username:branch`. Labels and reviewers are set via separate API calls after creation.

sections.title: Draft pull requests
sections.body: Set `draft: true` to create a work-in-progress PR that cannot be merged. Draft PRs still trigger CI checks from [[github/actions]] but signal to reviewers that the code is not ready. Convert from draft to ready-for-review with `gh pr ready 42` or `octokit.pulls.update({ draft: false })`.
